title: flyriella leonensis
intro: Flyriella leonensis, also known as African wild yam, is a plant species native to West Africa, particularly in the countries of Guinea, Sierra Leone, and Liberia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Flyriella leonensis include relief from menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Flyriella leonensis can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support reproductive and respiratory health, though more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
